# Glossary

Agent, Retex, Quality gate... AIDEN has its own vocabulary. Here's the dictionary — each term explained in one sentence.

---

## A

**ADR** — Architecture Decision Record. A document that records a significant architectural decision, generated in enterprise mode.

**Agent** — AI component specialized in a role (Lead, Analyst, Planner, Dev, QA, DocOps). Each agent extends `BaseAgent` and receives a provider from the router.

**Artifact** — Markdown file with YAML frontmatter generated by an agent (brief, spec, story, review, etc.). Stored in `.aiden/features/<slug>/`.

## B

**Brief** — Discovery document created by the Analyst. Summarizes the request, context, risks and constraints.

**Context bus** — Shared memory between all agents (`.aiden/memory/context.yaml`). Contains the detected tech stack, architecture and CI.

## C

**Circuit breaker** — Mechanism that stops an agent after N consecutive failures and escalates to a human. Default: 3 attempts.

**AIDEN** — AI-Driven Engineering. The framework itself.

## D

**Decisions log** — Append-only JSONL file that records all agent decisions. Protected by file locking.

## E

**Enterprise** — The most comprehensive mode with documentation, compliance and human approval between each phase.

## F

**Feature** — Unit of work in AIDEN. Each feature has its own lifecycle, artifact folder and state machine.

**Flash** — The fastest mode. Lead + Dev only, no spec or review. Ideal for hotfixes.

## L

**Lead** — Orchestrating agent that coordinates all other agents. Also detects the optimal mode via signal analysis.

## M

**Mode** — Workflow complexity level: flash, standard or enterprise. Determines which agents are executed and in what order.

## O

**OpenCode** — Alternative open source CLI provider. Configurable agentic tool pointing to any endpoint.

## P

**Provider** — External AI service (Claude Code, Gemini, OpenCode, Copilot or custom API) used by agents. Two adapter types: CLI (subprocess) and API (fetch).

## Q

**Quality gate** — Automatic verification between two workflow phases. Blocks progression if criteria are not met.

## R

**Retex** — Structured experience feedback (lessons learned). Stored in `.aiden/retex/` with context, lesson and recommendation. Injected into agent prompts under `--- Lessons Learned ---`.

**Routing** — Automatic selection of the optimal provider based on the task. Four strategies: `fixed`, `prefer`, `auto`, `cost-optimized`.

## S

**Sanitizer** — Filtering mechanism that masks sensitive data (tokens, API keys, passwords, connection strings) in prompts before sending to providers.

**Skill** — Declarative package (YAML + Markdown) in `.aiden/skills/` that injects instructions, rules and hooks into the AIDEN pipeline. The main extensibility mechanism for enterprises.

**Spec** — Lightweight technical specification created by the Planner. Contains requirements and acceptance criteria.

**Standard** — Intermediate mode with analysis, planning, development and review. The default choice.

**Story** — User story decomposed by the Planner with acceptance criteria. Numbered sequentially (001-*, 002-*).

## T

**Token budget** — Token limit per feature and per month to control costs. Configurable per mode (flash: 5000, standard: 20000, enterprise: 100000).
